D1 Optics L3 Apply Atom

Rayleigh Scattering Curve

slide the probe across the visible band and read scattering intensity at each wavelength

Rayleigh scattering rises sharply toward shorter wavelengths because the scattering cross-section grows as 1/λ⁴.

Slide the probe λ. Violet near 400 nm scatters about 9× harder than red near 700 nm — the raw mathematical reason the sky is blue.
